---
title: "Lecture 01 Notes: Introduction - A Roadmap to Autonomous Robotic Systems"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 01 slides](../slides/01-introduction-to-robotics.qmd)

## 1. Why robotics?

Robotics integrates mechanics, electronics, and computation to build embodied intelligent systems.

Key motivations:

- Safer operation in hazardous environments
- Improved precision and repeatability
- Scalable automation in logistics, healthcare, and manufacturing

## 2. Canonical robot architecture

A practical robot often includes:

- **Plant**: links, joints, motors, and transmission
- **Sensors**: encoders, IMU, camera, LiDAR, force sensors
- **Compute stack**: estimation, planning, and control modules
- **Communication layer**: middleware for real-time data flow

## 3. Mathematical foundations (preview)

For a manipulator with joint configuration \(q\):

- Forward kinematics: \(x = f(q)\)
- Differential kinematics: \(\dot{x} = J(q)\dot{q}\)

These models connect commanded joint motion to end-effector behavior.

## 4. Visualization workflow

Each lecture keeps code in a dedicated subdirectory under `code/`.

For lecture 01:

- [Code directory README](../code/lecture-01-introduction/README.md)
- [Workspace plotting script](../code/lecture-01-introduction/workspace_plot.py)

## 5. Reading checklist

- Define robotics in one sentence.
- Explain the role of each block in the robot pipeline.
- State the difference between \(f(q)\) and \(J(q)\dot{q}\).
